# Aetas Workplace

> Aetas Workplace is a business performance consultancy for UK SMEs, LLPs and professional practices. It helps organisations improve performance through leadership and people, employee wellbeing, financial wellbeing, employee benefits, culture, AI and technology, and commercial improvement. Every engagement begins with a no-cost Workplace Review. Delivered by Matthew Steiner and the Aetas team, supported by Aetas Wealth (FCA registration 458421).

Aetas Workplace is not a generic wellbeing platform or employee benefits provider. It is a consultancy-led engagement for organisations with between 10 and 250 employees, built around the Return on Employee Investment (ROEI) framework. Nothing is introduced without a Workplace Review first.

## Who this is for

- Business owners, managing directors, HR directors and finance directors in UK SMEs (10–250 employees) who want measurable improvements in productivity, engagement, retention and commercial performance
- LLPs and professional practices including law firms, accountancy practices, architecture and surveying firms, where performance improvement must not add overhead to the partnership
- Organisations where leadership, culture or commercial performance are as important as wellbeing and benefits

## Performance drivers

Aetas Workplace works across the drivers with the greatest measurable impact in each organisation. The Workplace Review identifies which apply:

**Leadership & People**, Leadership development, manager training, team performance

**Employee Wellbeing**, Workplace wellbeing, mental health, financial wellbeing

**Employee Benefits**, Benefits strategy, pensions, protection, reward

**Business Performance**, Performance reviews, AI and technology adoption, commercial improvement, Competitive Edge partner introductions

**Culture & Engagement**, Employer brand, engagement strategy, retention

## The ROEI Framework

ROEI (Return on Employee Investment) is the Aetas framework for measuring and improving the commercial return on an organisation's total investment in people. It covers salary, pension, benefits, recruitment, training, leadership, wellbeing and management time. The five stages are: Measure, Diagnose, Recommend, Implement, and Measure Again.

## The programme pillars

**Clarity**, a structured review of pension, benefits and financial support arrangements. Identifies gaps, inefficiencies and spend that is not performing.

**Confidence**, practical financial education for employees. Group workshops and optional one-to-one sessions with a regulated financial planner.

**Stability**, ongoing access to tools, guidance and partner support. Sustains improvements and builds long-term performance across the workforce.

## The team

- Matthew Steiner, Managing Director. Leads every Workplace Review personally. Over 20 years in financial services. matthew.steiner@aetas-wealth.com
- Daniel Cottam, Financial Planner. Delivers employee-facing workshops and one-to-one guidance sessions.
- Peter Rose APFS, Chartered Financial Planner and Pension Specialist. Specialist pension and financial planning support.

## The Aetas group

- **Aetas Workplace**, helps organisations improve business performance through people
- **Aetas Wealth**, personal financial planning for directors, business owners and senior professionals
- **Aetas Impact**, financial wellbeing, governance and resilience for charities and purpose-led organisations
- **Competitive Edge**, specialist delivery partner network across leadership, HR, technology, legal and commercial strategy

- [UK retail group](https://workplace.aetas-wealth.com/case-studies/uk-retail-group), diagnostic and benefits review completed. Shop St introduced as immediate employee benefit.

## Contact and booking

- Email: matthew.steiner@aetas-wealth.com
- [Book a Workplace Review](https://link.aetas-wealth.com/widget/form/9fkQAaigUnxLJQqOM17V), no cost, no obligation

## Regulatory

Aetas Workplace provides business performance consultancy, employee wellbeing, financial education and employee benefits services to employers. These services do not constitute regulated financial advice. Where regulated advice is required, this is provided separately by Aetas Wealth, a trading style of Insight Financial Associates Limited, authorised and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA registration: 458421). Registered office: 13 Hanover Square, Mayfair, London W1S 1HN.


## Detailed guide summaries

### The Cost of Financial Anxiety in the Workplace
Examines the direct commercial cost of employee financial stress. Covers productivity loss (estimated at one hour per financially stressed employee per day), absence data, management time impact, and the business case for intervention. Includes employer action checklist.

### Building an Employee Benefits Strategy for SMEs
Practical guide for SME employers on constructing a benefits package that employees value and use. Covers pension governance, salary sacrifice structures, group risk insurance, and low-cost high-impact additions. Includes a prioritisation framework.

### Salary Sacrifice Pensions Explained for Employers
Plain-English explanation of how salary sacrifice pension arrangements work, the National Insurance savings available to both employer and employee, implementation steps, and common employer questions answered.

### Employee Benefits and National Insurance Planning
How employers can reduce their National Insurance liability through benefits design, salary sacrifice arrangements and efficient structuring of total reward. Relevant following Finance Act 2026 changes to employer NI thresholds.

### Measuring the ROI of Financial Wellbeing Programmes
How to measure the return on a financial wellbeing investment. Covers baseline measurement, defining success metrics, tracking productivity and retention improvements, and presenting the business case internally.

### Generational Differences in the Workforce
How different generations experience financial pressure differently, and what this means for benefits design, communication strategy and programme delivery. Covers Gen Z, Millennial, Gen X and Baby Boomer financial priorities and concerns.
